name: Vo, Catherine
resolved_employer: Self-employed teacher/consultant (per filing; not independently verifiable)
evidence: Industry assigned via the categorize-by-employer fallback from the filing's own 'Teacher/Consultant, Self-Employed' occupation string; no public profile, school affiliation, consulting practice, or bio page could be matched to this specific person. 'Vo' is a very common Vietnamese surname and Austin has multiple same-named individuals, so identity is not independently corroborated beyond the filing. Pipeline data shows 114 FEC donations with a Democratic partisan lean, but itemized recipient detail could not be retrieved this run (FEC API rate-limited); no ideological-PAC hit surfaced in targeted name-vs-PAC searches.

name: Wainwright, Mike
evidence: Employer verified as a real Austin-area business: Pioneer Wine Company, L.P. is a wine and spirits wholesale distributor with an Austin location, distributing to retail, restaurant and bar accounts across Texas. Employer string and Austin 78722 residence are internally consistent, but no individual bio or company staff page confirming Mike Wainwright's role could be verified directly; other Austin-area Wainwrights found are distinct people. Pipeline data shows 90 FEC donations with a Democratic partisan lean; no ideological PAC recipients surfaced. The only Wainwright in TEC-adjacent lobbyist results was Dale Wainwright, a different person and not a registered lobbyist.

name: Holliman, Arnitta
resolved_employer: Cities United (national nonprofit reducing violence affecting Black men and boys) — Deputy Director; formerly Director, City of Milwaukee Office of Violence Prevention
evidence: Arnitta R. Holliman announced joining Cities United as Deputy in Jan 2025; previously appointed by Mayor Tom Barrett to lead Milwaukee's Office of Violence Prevention (2021-2022). Employer string 'Cities United' and occupation 'Deputy of Policy and Healing' match her documented role; Cities United is headquartered in Louisville with a distributed national staff, and she has publicly testified on Austin city violence-prevention policy.

affiliations.snippet: Holliman, Deputy Director of Cities United, appeared before Austin City Council supporting the resolution to strengthen the Office of Violence Prevention: 'Austin is taking a bold and necessary step, not just reacting to violence, but preventing it at the root.'

affiliations.org: City of Milwaukee Office of Violence Prevention
affiliations.snippet: Mayor Tom Barrett named Arnitta Holliman to lead the city's Office of Violence Prevention; she had previously served there as program manager for the Resiliency in Communities After Stress and Trauma effort.

A published VoyageDenver profile confirms she also works a full-time 9-to-5 job in the oil and gas industry alongside the auctioneering side business. That employment is rank-and-file, not executive/board/owner, and the one administrative-director title appearing in commercial data-broker listings is not confirmed by any primary source (she is not on the named company's published team page), so no oil_gas affiliation is recorded per the executive/board/owner standard.

Occupation 'Systems Analyst' and Austin-metro location (Cedar Park 78613) corroborate; the self-named employer string on the donation suggests self-reporting rather than a separate entity.

name: Philpot, Tasha
resolved_employer: University of Texas at Austin — Professor of Government; also founder of Philpot Consulting (academic/professional coaching)
evidence: Tasha S. Philpot is a Professor of Government at UT-Austin specializing in American politics, African-American politics and public opinion, with courtesy affiliations at UT's Center for African and African American Studies, Institute for Urban Policy Research & Analysis, and Center for Women's and Gender Studies. Name, employer string 'UT-Austin', occupation 'Professor' and Austin-metro zip all corroborate. University research-center affiliations are academic, not policy-advocacy roles, so none are recorded; no board seats or advocacy organization roles were found in her bio, personal site, or faculty page.

name: Countryman, Olivia
resolved_employer: Hilgers PLLC (Dallas office) — commercial litigation associate; profiled on the firm site as Olivia Justice
evidence: Olivia Justice Countryman is a commercial litigation associate in the Dallas office of Hilgers PLLC; Texas Bar licensed 2021, previously a litigation associate at Baker Botts and a law clerk to Judges John R. Parker and Sam A. Lindsay in the Northern District of Texas. Name, Dallas 75219 location and 'Lawyer'/'Hilgers PLLC' donation fields all corroborate. On the firm: Hilgers PLLC is the Lincoln, Nebraska-founded litigation boutique formerly known as Hilgers Graben PLLC, started in 2011 by Mike Hilgers — now Nebraska Attorney General, who ceased private practice in Dec 2022/Jan 2023 — and renamed in Nov 2025; it has no established connection to the Texas Attorney General's office. Countryman is a junior associate with no policy or government-affairs role. Her bio references unnamed 'non-profit board service'; no specific organization is identified in any public source, so no affiliation is recorded.

affiliations.snippet: Listed in Food Forward's Senior Leadership section as Chief Development Officer; Food Forward recovers surplus produce and distributes it to hunger-relief agencies across 13 California counties and seven adjacent states and tribal lands.

affiliations.role: Member of the Victory Campaign Board (Eastern Caucus Chair)

Byron LaMasters founded the Burnt Orange Report blog in 2003 and served as its editor through 2005.
